rulings.Case Title: EST. OF TEODORO DEL ROSARIO
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 9/27/2022 again same issues. Examiner continuances are final, not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B. 1) The amended settlement agreement must be filed as an amendment to the petition and 15 day's notice must be given. 2) The supplement alleges funds are being relocated to ensure no more than $250k is maintained in a single account. Please file current bank statements showing how the assets are currently held. 3) Page 15 of the petition under prayer for relief does not state how the estate is to be distributed to each distribute, including the net amount of cash (as a date certain) to be distributed to each beneficiary. [See Local Rule 14.35 I. 6)] 4) Please provide a revised proposed order to Examiner in room 202 as #9 does not appear to be accurate as the property was sold. The proposed order does not have amounts on page 5 lines 12, and page 6 as required by Local rule 14.35 I. 6) An endorsed-filed single sided hard copy of all documents filed in response to these notes, as well as any other documents required must be received by this Examiner in room 202 no later than 4 pm on 9/12/2022 [LRSF 14.7 B.]

rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #3, Report, Fees
rulings.Rulings: Hearing appearance required. Please review Local Rule 14.1 D. for information regarding remote appearances. To appear by Zoom, go to Zoom.com and click Join Meeting, and use the ID and password below. Meeting ID: 160 225 4765 Password: 514879 To appear by telephone through Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and follow the prompts, using the meeting ID and password above. Parties appearing should check in for the hearing using their first and last name. 1) Court to determine how to proceed. The investigator report filed 05/31/2022 alleges the conservator has replaced Ms. Ortiz's refrigerator, water heater, and furnace since the last review. The only repair in this account under disbursements is 1/19/22 Reinhardt Construction Solutions/Install garbage disposal and kitchen faucet for $585.00. The prior account does not show a new refrigerator purchased but does show a water heater/furnace. The 07/05/2022 Case Summary Report alleges conservatee does not have a working microwave or refrigerator. 2) Conservator requests $13,231.25 in fees as conservator of the estate as a 5% lien against the conservatee's real property. 3) Conservator requests $3,215.50 in attorney fees and $698.31 costs as a 5% lien against the conservatee's real property.

rulings.Case Title: CONSERVATORSHIP OF MARION HALL
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Fees
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 11/8/22. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: A "Supplemental Account of Conservatee's Personal Bank Account Prior to Conservatorship" was filed on 7/11/22. It is intended to be "incorporated" into the amended fist account, but no notice was given. It is unclear, however, what this "supplemental account" is intended to support. The copy of the bank statement attached to it is for the period 6/8/21 through 7/8/21; the co-conservators were appointed on 9/3/20, so the conservatee's bank statement immediately preceding 9/3/20 is required to be filed. [Prob. C. Sect. 2620(c)(2).] This "supplemental account" is -- again -- signed (verified) by just one of the two co-conservators when both are required to sign any pleading. [Probate Code Sect. 1020 and CRC 7.103(a) and (c).] Other issues posted in the tentative ruling for the hearing on 7/26/22 have not been addressed. Courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues and the issues posted for the 7/26/22 hearing must be received by the assigned Examiner by no later than 10/28/22. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF ROBERT BADELLA
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 09/20/2022. 1) No supplemental inventory and appraisal is on file (must be filed conventionally and cannot be e-filed). 2) The summary of account, assets on hand, and Schedule B (gains on sales) must be corrected based on the supplemental inventory and appraisal value of the car. 3) Approval of the funeral expenses ($8,685.67) as the personal representative's creditor claim must be specifically identified as relief in the petition caption and notice of the hearing. 4) The calculation of statutory fees appears incorrect. See Probate Code sections 10800(a)(4) & 10810(a)(4). 5) The total amount of reimbursement of expenses to the petitioner should be $5,268.91 (= $14,218.58 - $264 - $8,685.67). 6) Please clarify whether the assignees (American Research Bureau and Kemp & Associates) will receive direct distribution from the petitioner or through the assignors/beneficiaries. If direct distribution is intended, a specific amount of net cash distributable to the assignee and assignor must be separately stated. A revised proposed order should also be provided. The caption of the proposed order must begin with "Order for Final Distribution". LRSF 14.35(I)(6)(b). An amendment or amended petition must be filed to address some of the issues. Notice of the amendment or amended petition must be given on the mandatory Judicial Council form DE-120, with a copy of the remote appearance information required by LRSF 14.1(D)(6). Such notice cannot be given, however, until after the clerk has accepted and processed the amendment or amended petition, which occurs when the document becomes publicly viewable on the Court's register of actions. Note: Examiner continuances are final, not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10.B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF JACK JAY CARLSON III
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 10/25/22. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: No corrected (complete) Acknowledgment of Receipt of the Duties and Liabilities has been filed yet. The supplement was not filed in time to deliver a courtesy copy to the assigned examiner by the deadline of 8/19/22. A new Waiver of Bond filed on 8/17/22, ostensibly signed by the decedent's mother on 7/25/22, has a signature that is very different from her signature on the one attached to the petition filed on 2/8/22. Did the decedent's mother expressly consent to electronic service of notice? [CRC 2.251(b).] Courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues must be received by the assigned examiner by no later than 10/14/22 to avoid another continuance. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF DOUGLAS S. CHURCH
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 11/8/22. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: The will file has not yet been delivered to the assigned examiner; it appears from the register of actions that they were filed on 8/18/22. Item 1 of the amended petition is incomplete (name of newspaper). Item 3d of the petition does not make sense. If there is no personal property, how can there be $25,000 annual gross income from personal property? The date of the lost will appears to be 9/22/03, not 9/23/03. Both codicils refer to a will dated 4/30/09, not the one dated 9/22/03. The first codicil was dated by the testator on 11/19/09 but executed by the witnesses on 11/9/09. Item 5a(7) or (8) of the petition must be answered. Items 6 and 7 of the petition should not be answered since a spouse and issue survived. Item 8 of the petition must list ALL persons named in the will or any codicil and must clearly identify the trustee of devisee trust. If the petitioner is the trustee, all trust beneficiaries must also be listed, clearly identified as such, and must receive notice. The address of an interested party cannot be in care of another (e.g., his attorney) unless that person entitled to notice has directed in writing that notice be given to the second person. [CRC 7.51(a).] If an interested party is represented by an attorney, notice must ALSO be given to the attorney, but not ONLY to the attorney. The persons named in the will (Mishla Church (Schwartz) and Leslie Forsyth) are missing from item 8. They are also entitled to notice. [Probate Code Section 8110(b).] Is Leslie Forsyth the same person as Leslie Church? Courtesy copies of any pleadings filed to address these issues must be received by the assigned examiner by no later than 10/28/22. Courtesy copies are file stamped, single-sided copies. Copies of unfiled documents are recycled and not reviewed. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF BARBARA A. LEE
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION WITH WILL ANNEXED
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 9/27/2022 at request of counsel. Endorsed-filed single sided courtesy copies are required for any papers filed with the court and must be received by Examiner in room 202 at the Superior Court. Examiners will not review any document until after it has been filed and a courtesy copy has been received. [LRSF 2.7, 14.7]. Courtesy copies dropped off without a hearing date or examiner name on the envelope or a cover page, will be processed by the clerks as time allows and may cause delays. Petitioner must submit a hard copy of the proposed order to the Examiner in room 202 at least five (5) court days in advance of the scheduled hearing date. [LRSF 14.7]. Proposed orders dropped off without a hearing date or examiner name on the envelope or a cover page, will be processed by the clerks as time allows and may cause delays.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF HENRY NANG TOM
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 10/25/22. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: Item 5a(7) or (8) of the petition must be answered. Items 6 and 7 of the petition must be completed if no spouse or issue survived the decedent. When second generation or more distant heirs are listed, the deceased ancestor through whom they take (or would have taken) shall be named and his or her date of death and relationship to the decedent shall be stated. [LRSF 14.18 C.] Who were the decedent's parents and when did they die? (The relationship to the decedent of any person listed in item 8 of the petition should be stated in item 8, not on attachments or elsewhere.) Courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues must be received by the assigned Examiner by no later than 10/14/22. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.
